items.text:
NOTICE: 2026 Spring Semester Course Evaluations

All undergraduate students are required to complete course evaluations for each registered course before Friday, May 1, at 11:59 PM. Evaluations are accessed through the Student Portal under the "Evaluations" tab. The system will be available from Monday, April 13 through the deadline.

Important guidelines:
- Each evaluation takes approximately 8 to 12 minutes.
- Responses are anonymous; instructors cannot see individual submissions.
- A $5 bookstore credit will be applied automatically to each student who completes evaluations for all enrolled courses.
- Late submissions cannot be accepted under any circumstances.

Students who experience technical difficulties should contact the Registrar's Office at Anderson Hall, Room 204, between 9:00 AM and 4:30 PM on weekdays. Phone support is available at extension 1147. Submissions cannot be re-opened after the deadline closes, so early completion is strongly encouraged.

questions.stem: According to the notice, when does the evaluation system open?
questions.explanation: The notice states the system 'will be available from Monday, April 13 through the deadline.'

questions.stem: What is offered to students who complete all required course evaluations?

options.A: A waiver of next semester's registration fee.
options.B: A discount at the Registrar's Office shop.
options.C: A bookstore credit added to the student account.
options.D: A refund of any prior late submission charges.

questions.explanation: The notice states that 'A $5 bookstore credit will be applied automatically to each student who completes evaluations for all enrolled courses.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred from the notice about late submissions?

options.A: They may be filed up to 24 hours late.
options.B: They may be reviewed by the Registrar's Office staff.
options.C: They may receive partial bookstore credit on appeal.
options.D: They cannot be processed through any contact channel.

questions.explanation: The notice states late submissions 'cannot be accepted under any circumstances' and 'cannot be re-opened after the deadline,' implying no channel will accept them.

items.text:
NOTICE: Updated Lost and Found Policy

Effective Monday, September 8, the campus Lost and Found will operate under revised guidelines designed to handle increased volume.

Key changes:
- All items are now stored at Hammond Building, Room 110, instead of the Library Information Desk.
- Items are kept for 30 calendar days from the date of submission, after which unclaimed property is donated to local charities.
- Electronics and items valued over $50 will be logged with a confidential identifier and reported to Campus Security for safekeeping.
- A $2 administrative fee may be charged when claiming any item valued over $20.

To claim a lost item, students should bring a valid student ID and a brief description of the missing object. The Lost and Found counter is open Monday through Friday from 10:00 AM to 5:00 PM. For after-hours retrieval, contact Campus Security at extension 2300.

questions.stem: According to the notice, where are lost items now stored?

options.A: At the Library Information Desk on the first floor.
options.B: At Campus Security headquarters on weekends only.
options.C: At Hammond Building in Room 110 during open hours.
options.D: At the Registrar's Office during business hours daily.

questions.explanation: The notice states 'All items are now stored at Hammond Building, Room 110, instead of the Library Information Desk.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about an item that is not claimed within 30 days?

options.A: It is transferred to the Campus Security vault for safekeeping.
options.B: It is no longer available to its original owner.
options.C: It is auctioned online to cover administrative costs.
options.D: It is returned to the building where it was found.

questions.explanation: The notice states unclaimed property is 'donated to local charities' after 30 days, implying it is no longer available for the original owner to retrieve.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this notice?

options.A: To remind students that lost items must be claimed quickly.
options.B: To inform students about updated rules for lost-and-found services.
options.C: To announce a new charity partnership for unclaimed electronics.
options.D: To explain why Campus Security has expanded its protocols.

questions.explanation: The notice's headline 'Updated Lost and Found Policy' and the bulleted list of revised guidelines indicate the purpose is to communicate policy updates.
